subject: Wi-Fi shows connected but no internet
description: Menu bar shows Wi-Fi connected; browser and Teams fail to reach the internet.

ts_steps.title: Outage vs one Mac
ts_steps.action: Confirm another device on same SSID reaches internet. If all fail, NOC issue.

ts_steps.title: Captive portal
ts_steps.action: Safari http://captive.apple.com or any http site to trigger portal login.

ts_steps.title: VPN split tunnel
ts_steps.action: Disconnect VPN; retest. Reconnect and compare routes if intranet-only break.

ts_steps.title: Renew DHCP
ts_steps.action: System Settings → Network → Wi-Fi → Details → TCP/IP → Renew DHCP Lease.

ts_steps.title: DNS test
ts_steps.action: Details → DNS — note servers; add temporary test resolver if policy allows. Terminal: dscacheutil -flushcache; sudo killall -HUP mDNSResponder.

ts_steps.title: Ping router and beyond
ts_steps.action: Terminal: ping router IP; ping 1.1.1.1. If router OK but no internet, upstream or DNS.

ts_steps.title: Forget and rejoin SSID
ts_steps.action: Forget network, reconnect with correct credentials; accept 802.1x cert if prompted.

ts_steps.title: Proxies
ts_steps.action: Details → Proxies — ensure no stale manual proxy; PAC URL must be reachable.

ts_steps.title: Service order
ts_steps.action: System Settings → Network → … → Set Service Order — Wi-Fi above unused VPN interfaces if needed.

ts_steps.title: New location
ts_steps.action: Network → … → Edit Locations → + add test location with defaults; switch to it and retest before full rebuild.

internal_information: DHCP, DNS, PAC, captive portal, VPN. Apple Silicon Wi-Fi same user flows as modern macOS.
